run_summary: Screen 1 has three OVERDONE flags (BLKB, DUOL, PLAB) all confidence-3 with unjustified panic, plus one open PLAB position at days_held=0 showing early recovery. BLKB and DUOL warrant exploratory entries; PLAB position holds pending full sympathy-fade window.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI claims it solved an 80-year-old math problem — for real this time
entries.thesis: DUOL is the reflexive edtech sell on any OpenAI capability headline, but today's trigger is a math-reasoning demo, not a consumer language-learning product. DUOL's moat is the daily-habit dopamine/streak loop and gamification, not instructional content quality — areas a reasoning model breakthrough does not directly attack. The selloff is partial overshoot relative to the actual threat substance.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that an OpenAI consumer-facing tutor product is shipping with language-learning features; meaningful DAU decline in the next print; Apple/Google App Store featuring competing AI tutors prominently.
entries.reasoning: Confidence-3 OVERDONE but panic_calibration is 'partial', not 'unjustified'. While filings note GenAI risk, DUOL has launched Duolingo Max powered by GenAI, indicating integration rather than pure disruption. The trigger (OpenAI math-reasoning demo) has zero direct bearing on language-learning habit loops or gamification moat. However, 'partial' calibration and the fact that DUOL is actively hedging the threat means the sympathy-fade read is less clean than BLKB or PLAB. Watch for next data point (DAU, App Store ranking, or Gen-AI feature launch) before committing capital.
